setting.note: In Ferrara at the end of the 1930s, the narrator, a young Jewish man, is drawn into the walled world of the aristocratic Finzi-Contini family and their vast private garden, where he falls hopelessly in love with the elusive Micòl as the fascist race laws close a ring around them all. Bassani maps his own city: the great garden behind the walls off Corso Ercole I d'Este, the ramparts the young people cycle along, the synagogue on Via Mazzini, and the monumental Jewish cemetery where the book begins and the family's tomb waits.

rights.originalWork: In copyright. Giorgio Bassani, first published 1962 (Il giardino dei Finzi-Contini). Mapped as a cited work: no text stored, no passages reproduced. The places are facts and the notes are our own.
rights.textSource: None stored. A cited book reproduces no text.
rights.territoryNotes: Locations and original commentary only. No reproduction of the copyrighted text, in any territory.
rights.attribution: The Garden of the Finzi-Continis, Giorgio Bassani (1962). Locations mapped by Read the Places; not affiliated with or endorsed by the author's estate or publisher.

palette.note: The deep green of the enchanted walled garden, the huge trees and tennis lawn where a doomed world plays out its last summers. A cited book's colour is not a quotation, because we store no text to quote; it still has to be legible, and this clears WCAG AA on paper.
